data.title: Shell continues gains for eight straight sessions
data.description:
Shell (NYSE:SHEL) shares continued gains for eight straight sessions on Thursday, as the stock closed 0.5% higher at $71.88.

The oil giant gained over 7% in the preceding seven sessions. The stock has gained nearly 14% so far this year, compared to the over 3% fall in the broader S&P 500 Index.

SHEL is up 5% over the past one month. The stock closed 0.7% higher on Wednesday at $71.52.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, SHEL has a Hold rating with a score of 3.44 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it got a D+ in growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 14 analysts gave SHEL a Buy and above. Two analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and no one recommended Sell or lower.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also bullish and see the stock as a Buy.

Barclays analysts said investors have questioned Shell's ability to grow production, so management will want to demonstrate its portfolio is not in a long-run decline. The brokerage added that SHEL could boost its dividend per share by 10%-20% as early as 2026-27.

“Shell's undervaluation presents a rare opportunity for long-term investors, with potential for significant stock appreciation as market perception aligns with fundamentals,” argued Seeking Alpha analyst Motti Sapir.

Earlier in February, Shell forecast global demand for liquefied natural gas to rise by 60% by 2040, driven largely by economic growth in Asia, the impact of artificial intelligence, and efforts to cut emissions in heavy industries and transportation.

data.title: Micron ticks up as Q2 results, guidance top estimates on strong memory demand
data.description:
Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) shares rose 4.5% in late trading on Thursday after the memory maker reported second-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations amid strong demand for memory, due in part to artificial intelligence.

For the period ending Feb. 27, Micron said it earned an adjusted $1.56 per share as revenue rose 38% year-over-year to $8.05B. A consensus of analysts expected the company to earn $1.42 per share on $7.9B in revenue.

Adjusted gross margins during the period were 37.9%, while cash flow from operations came in at $3.94B. 

“Micron delivered fiscal Q2 EPS above guidance and data center revenue tripled from a year ago,” said Sanjay Mehrotra, Chairman, President and CEO of Micron Technology. “We are extending our technology leadership with the launch of our 1-gamma DRAM node. We expect record quarterly revenue in fiscal Q3, with DRAM and NAND demand growth in both data center and consumer-oriented markets, and we are on track for record revenue and significantly improved profitability in fiscal 2025.”

Looking ahead, Micron said it expects adjusted earnings to be between $1.47 and $1.67 per share, with the midpoint of $1.57 above the $1.52 per share estimate. Sales are forecast to be between $8.6B and $9B, with the midpoint well above the $8.48B estimate.

The company will host a conference call at 4:30 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: Bloom Energy breaks higher as Morgan Stanley raises price target to Street high
data.description:
Bloom Energy (NYSE:BE) +5.7% in Thursday's trading to its highest level in a month as Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco maintains his Overweight rating and raises his price target to a Street-high $35 from $28, calling it "the most underappreciated beneficiary of growing power demand in the U.S. and the accompanying need for distributed power generation solutions."

The hydrogen fuel cell company's technology will be "a key AI enabler as it is one of the key de-bottlenecking solutions for the power grid, which we estimate is facing a 42 GW shortfall" by 2028. the analyst writes.

After hosting company management for a series of meetings across Europe and at Morgan Stanley's Energy & Power and TMT conferences over the past two weeks, Percoco says he "came away with renewed confidence that Bloom Energy's technology will be pivotal to help fill the 42 GW power shortfall."

Bloom's (NYSE:BE) "ability to significantly improve its balance sheet and liquidity position, while continuously innovating its product to serve the AI/data center requirements, has positioned the
company particularly well to be the leading provider of time-to-power solutions for large-load customers," Percoco writes, adding that the recent 1 GW supply agreement with AEP is clear evidence of this "flywheel" taking effect, and other utilities and large customers likely will follow.

data.title: Lands' End shares fall after lackluster results, forecast
data.description:
Shares of Lands' End (NASDAQ:LE) fell as much as 13.3% during Thursday's trading session after the company provided a forecast that fell short of the average analyst expectation and reported fourth-quarter results that did not impress Wall Street.

The clothing retailer expects first-quarter revenue to be between $260M and $290M ($275M midpoint) and adjusted loss per share to be between $0.22 and $0.13 ($0.175 midpoint). The Bloomberg consensus estimate for revenue and non-GAAP LPS is $291.5M and $0.16, respectively.

For the full year, it expects revenue to be between $1.33B and $1.45B ($1.39B) and adjusted earnings per share to be between $0.48 and $0.86 ($0.67). The consensus estimate for revenue and non-GAAP EPS is $1.41B and $0.57, respectively. 

"Looking at 2025 and beyond, we are continuing to focus on generating improved cash flows, particularly from the prioritization of our licensing strategy and ongoing emphasis on more high-quality sales, which we expect will drive additional gross profit dollars and gross margin expansion over the long term,” the company said in a statement.

In Q4, the company saw gross merchandise value decrease in the low single digits compared to the fourth quarter of 2023. Gross margin rose about 760 bps to 45.6% vs. 38% last year. 

Cash and cash equivalents were $18.8M as of January 31, 2025, and net inventory was $265.1M.

Net income for the three months ended December 31 was 18.5M, or $0.59 per share, compared to a net loss of $8.6M, or a $0.27 loss per share, for the same period last year.

On an adjusted basis, the company earned 57 cents, in line with what analysts were expecting.

Revenue rose 14.2% to $441.9M and missed consensus estimates.

LE stock has lost nearly a third of its value in the last six months, while the benchmark S&P index declined 0.67%.

data.title: North American Construction Group targets $3.7-$4 adjusted EPS in 2025 while advancing Australian operations
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: North American Construction Group Ltd. (NOA) Q4 2024

Management View

  • The CEO highlighted record annual revenue for 2024, driven by strong Australian operations, including the MacKellar Group achieving its highest quarterly revenue. A record backlog of $3.5 billion was reported, supported by significant contract wins in Canada and Australia, including a four-year $500 million regional contract in the Canadian oil sands and a $100 million mining project in New South Wales.
  • The MacKellar acquisition has surpassed expectations, contributing to growth and diversification, with consistent fleet utilization at 82% in Q4 and plans to achieve 85% in early 2025. The Canadian fleet utilization improved to 54% and is expected to reach 75% by the end of 2025.
  • The CFO stated, "Headline EBITDA numbers of $104 million and 27.8% margin were driven by strong performances in Australia and operational improvements in the oil sands." Adjusted earnings per share for the quarter were $1.

Outlook

  • The company projects 2025 revenue between $1.4 billion and $1.6 billion, adjusted EBITDA of $415 million to $445 million, and adjusted EPS of $3.70 to $4.00. Free cash flow is expected to range from $130 million to $150 million, with sustaining capital at $180 million to $200 million.
  • Half-year and quarterly splits of EBITDA are expected to be weighted towards H2 2025, with Q1 contributing less than Q2 and Q3 being the largest quarter.
  • The geographic focus remains on diversifying beyond Queensland and Alberta, with continued expansion in Australian operations and infrastructure projects targeted to represent 25% of earnings over the next few years.

Financial Results

  • Q4 EBITDA of $104 million reflected a 27.8% margin, with strong contributions from Australia and a stable oil sands business. Combined gross profit margin, excluding adjustments, was 19.7%.
  • Free cash flow for Q4 was $50 million, driven by strong EBITDA performance and lower capital spending.
  • Net debt decreased to $856 million, with subsequent debt conversion reducing it further to $783 million. Leverage ended at 2.0 times net debt.

Q&A

  • Yuri Lynk, Canaccord Genuity, raised concerns about Canadian fleet utilization and oil sands market trends. The CEO emphasized ongoing efforts to improve utilization to 75% by 2025 and noted steady demand for large equipment despite reduced civil construction activity.
  • Aaron MacNeil, TD Cowen, inquired about mobilizing equipment for Australian projects. The CEO confirmed the continuation of asset transfers from Canada to Australia for improved returns.
  • Maxim Sytchev, National Bank Financial, asked about infrastructure expansion. Management indicated a focus on capital-light infrastructure projects in the U.S. and Australia, emphasizing climate-resilient projects.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was cautiously optimistic, emphasizing concerns about Canadian utilization and oil sands market dynamics. Questions reflected interest in diversification and growth strategies.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, highlighting strategic achievements and future growth opportunities. Statements like "we believe we're in the best position we've ever been" indicated strong confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone showed increased focus on addressing challenges in Canada while maintaining optimism about Australian operations.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue guidance and adjusted EPS projections for 2025 were newly introduced, providing clearer forward-looking insights.
  • Strategic focus shifted towards leveraging Australian operations and infrastructure opportunities.
  • Analysts maintained similar concerns about oil sands dynamics, while management showed consistent confidence in addressing these challenges.

Risks and Concerns

  • The Canadian oil sands market remains a challenge, with reduced civil construction activity and competitive pressures. Management plans to address this through asset reallocation and targeted project wins.
  • Weather impacts in Australia during Q1 2025 were noted, but management expressed confidence in mitigating these through operational adjustments.
  • Debt levels and interest rates are being closely monitored, with commitments to leverage reduction and disciplined capital allocation.

Final Takeaway

North American Construction Group reported strong growth driven by Australian operations and diversification efforts. With record backlog, clear 2025 guidance, and strategic initiatives focused on infrastructure and geographic expansion, the company is confident in its ability to sustain profitability and free cash flow growth. Challenges in the Canadian oil sands market remain, but management's adaptive strategies and focus on high-utilization opportunities position the company well for continued success.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Coty upgraded at Citi on attractive valuation, bullish medium-term outlook
data.description:
The 38% correction in Coty (NYSE:COTY) shares over the past six months and FY25 estimate revisions now fully reflects the weakness in the consumer beauty segment, presenting a buying opportunity in the stock, says Citi Research analyst Filippo Falomi in Thursday’s note to clients.

Additionally, the sale of its Wella business presents a large value-unlock opportunity, which Coty (NYSE:COTY) targets for the end of this year.

“With valuation already reflecting concerns, we upgrade to Buy [from Hold] and raise our target price to $8 on ~8x CY25 estimated EBITDA,” adds Falomi.

Coty’s (NYSE:COTY) topline growth has decelerated from 10%/11% growth in FY23/FY24 to just +1.7% in the first half of FY25 as the consumer beauty business slowed on low-income consumer pressure and inventory destocking, and the prestige fragrance business normalized after a few years of double-digit growth.

Medium-term, however, Coty’s (COTY) outlook is still attractive, with Citi targeting 3% to 7% category growth. While the reported topline is likely to be below those rates in the second half of this year and into the first half of 2026 on retailer inventory reductions, Falomi sees a potential for re-acceleration through FY26.

And despite softer topline trends, consumer demand for Coty’s (COTY) brands remains healthy, with prestige fragrance the fastest-growing sub-segment of the beauty category.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors share a bullish view of Coty (COTY) with a consensus Buy rating. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, however, views Coty (COTY) as a Strong Sell, earning a Quant score of 1.48 out of 5.

data.title: Destination XL outlines growth strategies amid challenging retail conditions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Destination XL Group, Inc. (DXLG) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Harvey Kanter acknowledged challenging Q4 results, citing sector headwinds and greater market volatility impacting consumer behavior in the men’s big and tall apparel segment. He emphasized ongoing efforts to execute the company’s long-range strategic plan, including store expansion, e-commerce upgrades, and marketing campaigns.
  • The company opened seven new stores and converted eight Casual Male locations to DXL stores in 2024, improving accessibility for customers. Additionally, a new e-commerce platform and loyalty program were introduced to enhance customer engagement and drive sales.
  • CFO Peter Stratton reported Q4 sales of $119.2 million, impacted by an 8.7% decline in comparable sales. Merchandise margins improved by 50 basis points due to product mix shifts, reduced shipping costs, and lower loyalty program expenses.
  • CEO Kanter highlighted initiatives like the Fit Exchange program and partnerships with brands like Nordstrom and TravisMathew to strengthen market presence. He emphasized the company’s solid financial footing with no debt and $48.4 million in cash.

Outlook

  • The company refrained from issuing full-year 2025 guidance, citing market volatility and macroeconomic uncertainties. It expects comparable sales to improve, moving from low-double-digit declines in Q1 to positive results in the second half of 2025.
  • Management aims to stabilize performance by focusing on cost control, targeted promotions, customer acquisition, and selective store development. Strategic initiatives for 2025 include expanding the FitMAP technology and enhancing e-commerce operations.

Financial Results

  • Q4 sales were $119.2 million, with an 8.7% decline in comparable sales. Store sales were down 6.7%, while direct sales dropped 12.7%.
  • Merchandise margins improved by 50 basis points year-over-year, supported by strategic promotions and inventory management. Gross margin, inclusive of occupancy costs, decreased to 44.4% from 47% in Q4 2023 due to deleveraging.
  • SG&A expenses were 41.7% of sales, reflecting a decrease in marketing costs and incentive accruals. Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $4.2 million, or 3.5% of sales.
  • The company ended 2024 with $48.4 million in cash and $1.9 million in positive free cash flow after capital expenditures.

Q&A

  • Michael Baker, D.A. Davidson: Questioned the impact of GLP-1 weight loss drugs on the company’s customer base. CEO Kanter acknowledged some customers are dropping out of the size range but highlighted initiatives like the Fit Exchange program to attract and retain customers.
  • Jeremy Hamblin, Craig-Hallum: Asked about tariff exposures and promotions. CFO Stratton noted minimal tariff impact due to sourcing strategies, while CEO Kanter discussed targeted promotions to drive traffic and acquisition.
  • Analysts also inquired about the impact of new store openings and loyalty program changes. CEO Kanter indicated that traffic challenges are the primary issue for new stores, and the revamped loyalty program aims to enhance customer engagement.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns over declining traffic, promotional activity, and broader market conditions. Their tone was cautiously optimistic about the company’s strategic initiatives.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing financial stability and strategic progress. However, responses in the Q&A reflected a more defensive stance, particularly on tariff impacts and store performance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remained focused on navigating challenges while executing long-term strategies.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from providing specific fiscal year expectations to focusing on stabilization efforts for 2025, reflecting increased caution amid market uncertainties.
  • Management’s confidence in strategic initiatives, such as e-commerce upgrades and the FitMAP technology, remained consistent, but emphasis on promotional activities increased.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted towards understanding the impact of external factors like weight loss drugs and tariffs.

Risks and Concerns

  • Persistent traffic declines and consumer price sensitivity in the men’s big and tall apparel market.
  • Potential tariff impacts on gross margins and pricing strategies.
  • Challenges in ramping up new store performance and building brand awareness.
  • Dependence on strategic promotions and loyalty program effectiveness to drive sales.

Final Takeaway

Destination XL Group faced significant challenges in Q4 2024, with declining sales and consumer uncertainty in the men’s big and tall apparel sector. Despite these headwinds, the company emphasized its solid financial position and strategic initiatives aimed at driving long-term growth. Key focus areas include enhancing e-commerce capabilities, expanding store presence selectively, and leveraging partnerships to increase brand awareness and customer engagement. The outlook for 2025 reflects cautious optimism as the company navigates a volatile market environment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Abeona Therapeutics targets $2B revenue potential for pz-cel amid U.S. launch preparations
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Abeona Therapeutics (ABEO) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Vish Seshadri highlighted the upcoming FDA decision on prademagene zamikeracel (pz-cel), scheduled for April 29, 2025, as a pivotal milestone. The company is preparing for a potential launch in Q3 2025, with expectations that pz-cel will become a significant driver of growth and profitability.
  • Seshadri stated that the FDA has provided a marked-up draft label for pz-cel and has not required additional site inspections. Launch preparations include onboarding five renowned EB treatment centers to be qualified treatment centers (QTCs).
  • Chief Commercial Officer Madhav Vasanthavada projected that pz-cel could generate over $2 billion in cumulative revenue in the U.S., based on an estimated 1,500 treatment opportunities for the 750 eligible RDEB patients.
  • CFO Joe Vazzano disclosed that Abeona had $98.1 million in cash and equivalents as of December 31, 2024, providing financial resources to fund operations into 2026 without considering potential pz-cel revenues or a Priority Review Voucher (PRV) sale.

Outlook

  • Management anticipates pz-cel's commercial launch in Q3 2025, pending FDA approval. Five treatment centers are expected to be activated within three months post-approval.
  • Vasanthavada emphasized market readiness, stating that commercial payers covering 60%-65% of RDEB patients have shown positive feedback on pz-cel's clinical value. The company plans to scale manufacturing capacity to address demand.

Financial Results

  • Abeona reported a full-year net loss of $63.7 million for 2024, compared to $54.2 million in 2023. Research and development expenses increased to $34.4 million, primarily due to commercial launch preparation costs.
  • General and administrative expenses rose to $29.9 million, reflecting investments in building a commercial team and other launch preparations.

Q&A

  • Kristen Kluska, Cantor: Asked about FDA satisfaction regarding the resolution of CMC-related issues. Seshadri stated that the company has addressed all items from the CRL and believes it is in a strong position.
  • François Brisebois, Oppenheimer: Asked about physician education and patient community awareness. Vasanthavada noted efforts to onboard key treatment centers and leverage patient advocacy groups for awareness campaigns.
  • Maury Raycroft, Jefferies: Inquired about initial patient treatment volumes and manufacturing capacity. Seshadri confirmed the initial manufacturing capacity at launch would support up to four treatments per month, scaling to 10 treatments per month by 2026.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism about FDA approval and pz-cel's market potential. Questions primarily focused on the regulatory process, manufacturing capacity, and commercial strategy.
  • Management maintained a confident and forward-looking tone, highlighting progress in addressing regulatory requirements and preparing for a successful product launch.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter's focus shifted to commercial readiness and revenue potential for pz-cel, compared to the previous quarter's emphasis on regulatory progress.
  • Management's tone remained confident, with increased detail on market access strategies and manufacturing scale-up plans.
  • Analysts' questions evolved from regulatory concerns to operational execution and market dynamics.

Risks and Concerns

  • Key risks include FDA approval dependency, manufacturing scale-up challenges, and potential delays in treatment center onboarding.
  • Management highlighted mitigation strategies, including early engagement with treatment centers and proactive payer education to ensure reimbursement readiness.

Final Takeaway

Abeona Therapeutics is positioning itself for a transformative 2025, with pz-cel expected to drive significant growth upon potential FDA approval. The company's strategic focus on market preparation, manufacturing capacity expansion, and payer engagement underpins its confidence in capturing a substantial share of the RDEB treatment market.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Freeport McMoRan raised at J.P. Morgan, as tariff risk seen maintaining premium pricing
data.description:
Freeport McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) +0.8% in Thursday's trading as J.P. Morgan upgrades shares to Overweight from Neutral with a $52 price target, raised from $48 previously, saying tariff risk likely will maintain premium pricing for the company's U.S.-based footprint for the foreseeable future, combined with expected long-term supply challenges supporting a favorable through-cycle pricing environment for the industry.

Since President Trump said on February 25 that the Commerce Department would review whether U.S. copper imports pose a national security risk, Comex copper has averaged a 10% premium to LME copper, which should directly benefit Freeport's (NYSE:FCX) exposure, amounting to ~10% of FY 2024 operating profit and an even greater 20% of post-tax earnings given NOLs.

JPM analyst Bill Peterson believes Trump 2.0 trade policy to date infers it is more likely than not that some form of protectionism for U.S. copper imports unfolds, but if not, Freeport (FCX) is "still advantageously positioned relative to pure-play peers to capture incremental upside in the near term," with 2025 mine supply growth forecast decelerating to 1% from 3% last year.

Over the longer term, Peterson sees energy transition demand and lagging mine supply deepening current deficits and supporting higher through-the-cycle copper pricing; taken together with easing capex next year, the analyst sees Freeport (FCX) generating a significant amount of additional free cash flow, which can support the company’s base and performance-
based payout framework.

data.title: SA Asks: What's next for EV automaker BYD?
data.description:
EV maker BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) has been on a roll.

Earlier this week, the Chinese company made headlines by unveiling a five-minute charging system that can fuel an EV in about the same time as it would take to fill a traditional car with gas. The news came just a few weeks after BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) announced plans to make its God’s Eye driver-assistance system available in most of its vehicles at no extra charge.

Year-to-date, BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) shares have risen 60%, compared with the S&P 500, which has slipped 3.4%, as of Thursday afternoon. 

So what's next for BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF)? Seeking Alpha analysts The Techie and Danil Sereda gave us their thoughts on what's ahead for the carmaker. 

The Techie: BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) not only holds pricing power against Tesla (TSLA), Nio (NIO), Li Auto (LI), and XPeng (XPEV), but it’s also consistently reminding us that it holds the edge in innovation, which isn’t just bound to cars.  

On March 17th, BYD (BYDFF) released the 10C megawatt charging station and the world’s first charger with 1000V and 1000A that can charge 400 km in five minutes. The 1000 kW charger, called the Megawatt Flash Charger, proves rivals are lagging behind, with Tesla (TSLA) V4 at 500 kW, Li Auto (LI)  5C at 520 kW, Nio (NIO) Power at 640 kW and XPeng (XPEV) S5 at 800 kW.  

The company has more than doubled its market cap since 2023, and I think we could see it grow two-fold over the next five years, especially with its advantage of economies of scale.  

BYD’s (OTCPK:BYDDF) global expansion efforts aren’t exempt from EU tariffs, at 27.4% now, or delays in Mexico's plant approval. Still, BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) stands out from Tesla (TSLA) and the allies by offering innovation at a lower price point. On the other hand, it stands out from the Chinese bunch by being the most affordable with a consistent supply of new products. I think it's the best positioned to mitigate the risks. 

Danil Sereda:  Generally speaking, I'm neutral about the stock if we consider it a potential buying opportunity because of the elevated valuation levels. Of course, BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) is too far from Tesla (TSLA) in this regard, but still - paying 25x+ forwarding earnings for a Chinese stock is a bit too much for me.  

The company itself is doing a fantastic job at business expansion amid strict cost management that helps it save a ton of money where others fail, including global rival Tesla (TSLA). Local competitors such as Li (LI), Xpeng (XPEV) and Nio (NIO) seem to be lagging behind in terms of selling volumes, pricing power and technology.

BYD's (OTCPK:BYDDF) recent announcement of ultra-fast charging, although it's likely a long process for wide usage, gives them yet another edge in the market, where the vertical integration of their business structure allows them to keep prices relatively low.  

With five-minute charging, EV adoption in China and nearby countries will likely accelerate even further, and so BYD's (OTCPK:BYDDF) earnings growth visibility has now become clearer, in my view. Hence, the valuation premium. But again, I'd wait for a better price.
